publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Iteration; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Iteration: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? A loop terminates automatically once its body has run enough times, even if the condition never changes.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. Identify and test the progress measure on every path, then prove that the continuation condition eventually becomes false or input is exhausted.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. Identify and test the progress measure on every path, then prove that the continuation condition eventually becomes false or input is exhausted.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Iteration instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: Identify and test the progress measure on every path, then prove that the continuation condition eventually becomes false or input is exhausted.
A loop terminates automatically once its body has run enough times, even if the condition never changes.

answer.explanation: Identify and test the progress measure on every path, then prove that the continuation condition eventually becomes false or input is exhausted.
